item.title: [7] Gigantic Nonreciprocal Conduction at a Polar-Magnetic Interface of GdTiO3/EuTiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Y. Tokura, M. Kawasaki

Authors: N. Takahara, K. S. Takahashi, N. Nagaosa, Y. Tokura, M. Kawasaki

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Interesting transport study in magnetic oxide heterostructures with broken symmetry.

Tags: #transport #heterostructure #magnetic

Abstract: Author(s): N. Takahara, K. S. Takahashi, N. Nagaosa, Y. Tokura, and M. Kawasaki Oxide heterostructures provide excellent playgrounds to investigate novel transport phenomena emerging at interfaces due to broken inversion symmetry, in stark contrast to constituent compounds. Here, we fabricate ${\mathrm{GdTiO}}_{3}/{\mathrm{EuTiO}}_{3}$ heterostructures, where a two-dimensional … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206304] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Y. Tokura, M. Kawasaki

Authors: N. Takahara, K. S. Takahashi, N. Nagaosa, Y. Tokura, M. Kawasaki

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Interesting transport study in magnetic oxide heterostructures with broken symmetry.

Tags: #transport #heterostructure #magnetic

Abstract: Author(s): N. Takahara, K. S. Takahashi, N. Nagaosa, Y. Tokura, and M. Kawasaki Oxide heterostructures provide excellent playgrounds to investigate novel transport phenomena emerging at interfaces due to broken inversion symmetry, in stark contrast to constituent compounds. Here, we fabricate ${\mathrm{GdTiO}}_{3}/{\mathrm{EuTiO}}_{3}$ heterostructures, where a two-dimensional … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206304] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Gigantic Nonreciprocal Conduction at a Polar-Magnetic Interface of GdTiO3/EuTiO3

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Photoinduced Metal-to-Insulator Transitions in 2D Moiré Devices
item.description:
Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu

Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of correlated phases in moiré systems using ultrafast techniques.

Tags: #moire #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, Birui Yang, Dihao Sun, Luke Holtzman, Juseung Oh, Katayun Barmak, Cory R. Dean, James C. Hone, Nathaniel Gabor, Eric A. Arsenault, and Xiaoyang Zhu A laser-based approach rapidly injects charge into moiré materials and drives metal-to-insulator transitions. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206502] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu

Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of correlated phases in moiré systems using ultrafast techniques.

Tags: #moire #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, Birui Yang, Dihao Sun, Luke Holtzman, Juseung Oh, Katayun Barmak, Cory R. Dean, James C. Hone, Nathaniel Gabor, Eric A. Arsenault, and Xiaoyang Zhu A laser-based approach rapidly injects charge into moiré materials and drives metal-to-insulator transitions. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206502] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Photoinduced Metal-to-Insulator Transitions in 2D Moiré Devices
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu; Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Correlation-Driven Ultrafast Exciton Diffusion in Hubbard-Regime Moiré Superlattices
item.description:
Last authors: Jianbin Luo, Dameng Liu

Authors: Huan Liu, Haowen Xu, Shihong Chen, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental investigation of correlated phases in Hubbard-regime moiré lattices.

Tags: #moire #Hubbard #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Huan Liu, Haowen Xu, Shihong Chen, Rui Han, Zejun Sun, Mingxin Xu, Shuchun Huang, Xiushuo Zhang, Li Huang, Jianbin Luo, and Dameng Liu Ultrafast microscopy reveals that exciton flow in moiré superlattices can be switched on or off by competing Hubbard interactions and correlated electron phases, enabling quantum control of energy transport. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206302] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Jianbin Luo, Dameng Liu

Authors: Huan Liu, Haowen Xu, Shihong Chen, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental investigation of correlated phases in Hubbard-regime moiré lattices.

Tags: #moire #Hubbard #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Huan Liu, Haowen Xu, Shihong Chen, Rui Han, Zejun Sun, Mingxin Xu, Shuchun Huang, Xiushuo Zhang, Li Huang, Jianbin Luo, and Dameng Liu Ultrafast microscopy reveals that exciton flow in moiré superlattices can be switched on or off by competing Hubbard interactions and correlated electron phases, enabling quantum control of energy transport. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 206302] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Correlation-Driven Ultrafast Exciton Diffusion in Hubbard-Regime Moiré Superlattices

item.title: [7] Spin-Galvanic Response to Nonequilibrium Spin Injection in Superconductors with Spin-Orbit Coupling
item.description:
Last authors: Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret

Authors: I. V. Tokatly, Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of spin transport in superconductors relevant to spintronics/superconductivity.

Tags: #superconductivity #spin-orbitcoupling

Abstract: Author(s): I. V. Tokatly, Yao Lu, and F. Sebastian Bergeret We show that nonequilibrium spin injection into a superconductor can generate an anomalous supercurrent or induce a phase gradient, even for spin voltages below the superconducting gap. Our mechanism does not require breaking time-reversal symmetry in the effective superconducting Hamiltonian, but i… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196303] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret

Authors: I. V. Tokatly, Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of spin transport in superconductors relevant to spintronics/superconductivity.

Tags: #superconductivity #spin-orbitcoupling

Abstract: Author(s): I. V. Tokatly, Yao Lu, and F. Sebastian Bergeret We show that nonequilibrium spin injection into a superconductor can generate an anomalous supercurrent or induce a phase gradient, even for spin voltages below the superconducting gap. Our mechanism does not require breaking time-reversal symmetry in the effective superconducting Hamiltonian, but i… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196303] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Spin-Galvanic Response to Nonequilibrium Spin Injection in Superconductors with Spin-Orbit Coupling
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret; Authors: I. V. Tokatly, Yao Lu, F. Sebastian Bergeret

item.title: [8] Spinless and Spinful Charge Excitations in Moiré Fractional Chern Insulators
item.description:
Last authors: B. Andrei Bernevig, Nicolas Regnault

Authors: Miguel Gonçalves, Juan Felipe Mendez-Valderrama, Jonah Herzog-Arbeitman, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Microscopic theory for moire fractional Chern insulators, highly relevant to topological materials.

Tags: #Chern #moire #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Miguel Gonçalves, Juan Felipe Mendez-Valderrama, Jonah Herzog-Arbeitman, Jiabin Yu, Xiaodong Xu, Di Xiao, B. Andrei Bernevig, and Nicolas Regnault Large-scale exact diagonalization yields the first direct microscopic computation of spinless and spinful quasiparticle charge gaps in a fractional Chern insulator. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196503] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: B. Andrei Bernevig, Nicolas Regnault

Authors: Miguel Gonçalves, Juan Felipe Mendez-Valderrama, Jonah Herzog-Arbeitman, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Microscopic theory for moire fractional Chern insulators, highly relevant to topological materials.

Tags: #Chern #moire #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Miguel Gonçalves, Juan Felipe Mendez-Valderrama, Jonah Herzog-Arbeitman, Jiabin Yu, Xiaodong Xu, Di Xiao, B. Andrei Bernevig, and Nicolas Regnault Large-scale exact diagonalization yields the first direct microscopic computation of spinless and spinful quasiparticle charge gaps in a fractional Chern insulator. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196503] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Spinless and Spinful Charge Excitations in Moiré Fractional Chern Insulators

item.title: [9] Fingerprints of Preformed Pairs in Two-Electron Angle-Resolved Photoemission Spectroscopy
item.description:
Last authors: Andrea Damascelli, Mona Berciu

Authors: Janez Bonča, Andrea Damascelli, Mona Berciu

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: angle-resolved photoemission

Tags: #ARPES #angle-resolvedphotoemission #photoemission #Hubbard

Abstract: Author(s): Janez Bonča, Andrea Damascelli, and Mona Berciu We use variational exact diagonalization (VED) to calculate the two-electron removal spectral weight for the Hubbard-Holstein model, starting from the ground state with two electrons on a one-dimensional chain. We argue that this spectral weight provides a valuable proxy for the intensity of 2eARPES… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196504] Published Thu May 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Andrea Damascelli, Mona Berciu

Authors: Janez Bonča, Andrea Damascelli, Mona Berciu

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: angle-resolved photoemission

Tags: #ARPES #angle-resolvedphotoemission #photoemission #Hubbard

Abstract: Author(s): Janez Bonča, Andrea Damascelli, and Mona Berciu We use variational exact diagonalization (VED) to calculate the two-electron removal spectral weight for the Hubbard-Holstein model, starting from the ground state with two electrons on a one-dimensional chain. We argue that this spectral weight provides a valuable proxy for the intensity of 2eARPES… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196504] Published Thu May 14, 2026

item.title: [7] Optimally Tensile Strained La3Ni2O7 Films as Candidate High-Temperature Superconductors on Designer Substrates Ba1−xSrxO and SrO-Terminated SrTiO3
item.description:
Last authors: Yu Jia, Zhenyu Zhang

Authors: Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: First-principles calculations on nickelate superconductors, a high-interest material class for unconventional superconductivity.

Tags: #Nickelates #Superconductivity #DFT

Abstract: Author(s): Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, Shuo Cai, Huafeng Dong, Yu Jia, and Zhenyu Zhang High-temperature superconductivity in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$-derived films with critical temperatures (${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$) of 40–50 K has so far been realized only under substrate-induced compressive strain. Here we use first-principles calculations to predict that such… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196002] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yu Jia, Zhenyu Zhang

Authors: Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: First-principles calculations on nickelate superconductors, a high-interest material class for unconventional superconductivity.

Tags: #Nickelates #Superconductivity #DFT

Abstract: Author(s): Liangliang Liu, Junhao Peng, Zhuangzhuang Qiao, Shuo Cai, Huafeng Dong, Yu Jia, and Zhenyu Zhang High-temperature superconductivity in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$-derived films with critical temperatures (${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$) of 40–50 K has so far been realized only under substrate-induced compressive strain. Here we use first-principles calculations to predict that such… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196002] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Optimally Tensile Strained La3Ni2O7 Films as Candidate High-Temperature Superconductors on Designer Substrates Ba1−xSrxO and SrO-Terminated SrTiO3

item.title: [9] Twist and Strain in Square Moiré Patterns of Stacked Graphene Layers
item.description:
Last authors: Francisco Guinea, Iván Brihuega

Authors: Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: STM study of strain-induced moiré patterns in graphene, directly matching user's methodology and materials.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #STM #Graphene #Moiré

Abstract: Author(s): Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, Eva Cortés-del Río, Beatriz Viña-Bausá, Yulia Maximenko, Pierre A. Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Iván Brihuega We report the first observation of controlled, strain-induced square moiré patterns in stacked graphene. By selectively displacing native wrinkles, we drive a reversible transition from the usual trigonal to square moiré order. Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals elliptically shaped AA domains, wh… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196102] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Francisco Guinea, Iván Brihuega

Authors: Roberto Carrasco, Federico Escudero, Zhen Zhan, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Pantaleón, Francisco Guinea, and Iván Brihuega We report the first observation of controlled, strain-induced square moiré patterns in stacked graphene. By selectively displacing native wrinkles, we drive a reversible transition from the usual trigonal to square moiré order. Scanning tunneling microscopy reveals elliptically shaped AA domains, wh… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196102] Published Tue May 12, 2026

item.title: [7] Breakdown of the Wiedemann-Franz Law in an Interacting Quantum Hall Metamaterial
item.description:
Last authors: François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Authors: Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of thermal transport and Wiedemann-Franz law violation in a quantum Hall system.

Tags: #QuantumHall #Transport

Abstract: Author(s): Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, and Olivier Maillet Interacting quantum Hall metamaterials violate the Wiedemann-Franz law, with a Lorenz ratio scaling as the square root of the chain length. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196301] Published Tue May 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Authors: Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, Olivier Maillet

Abstract: Author(s): Patrice Roche, Carles Altimiras, François D. Parmentier, and Olivier Maillet Interacting quantum Hall metamaterials violate the Wiedemann-Franz law, with a Lorenz ratio scaling as the square root of the chain length. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196301] Published Tue May 12, 2026

item.title: [9] Fragility of Topology under Electronic Correlations in Iron Chalcogenides
item.description:
Last authors: Minjae Kim, Changyoung Kim

Authors: Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Investigation of correlations and topology in iron chalcogenides by a prominent spectroscopy group.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #IronChalcogenide #Topology #Correlations

Abstract: Author(s): Younsik Kim, Junseo Yoo, Sehoon Kim, Sungsoo Hahn, Kiyohisa Tanaka, Li Yu, Minjae Kim, and Changyoung Kim The interplay between electronic correlations and topology is a central topic in the study of quantum materials. In this Letter, we investigate the impact of the orbital-selective Mott phase (OSMP) on the topological properties of ${\mathrm{FeTe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ (FTS), an iron chalcogenide … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196502] Published Tue May 12, 2026

In this Letter, we investigate the impact of the orbital-selective Mott phase (OSMP) on the topological properties of ${\mathrm{FeTe}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Se}}_{x}$ (FTS), an iron chalcogenide … [Phys. item.title: [9] Light-Induced Charge Order Mode in a Metastable Cuprate Ladder
item.description:
Last authors: Yao Wang, Matteo Mitrano

Authors: Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Observation of emergent charge order in a cuprate, highly relevant to user's CDW and RIXS interests.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #Cuprates #CDW #Ultrafast

Abstract: Author(s): Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, Filippo Glerean, Antoine Roll, Pan Zhou, Sarbajaya Kundu, Arnau Romaguera, Elizabeth Skoropata, Hiroki Ueda, Biaolong Liu, Eugenio Paris, Yu Wang, Seng Huat Lee, Zhiqiang Mao, Mark P. M. Dean, Edwin W. Huang, Elia Razzoli, Yao Wang, and Matteo Mitrano We report the observation of an emergent charge order mode in the optically excited cuprate ladder ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{14}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{24}{\mathrm{O}}_{41}$. Near-infrared light in the ladder plane drives a symmetry-protected electronic metastable state together with a partial melting of the equilibri… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196501] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yao Wang, Matteo Mitrano

Authors: Hari Padma, Prakash Sharma, Sophia F. R. TenHuisen, et al.

Dean, Edwin W. Huang, Elia Razzoli, Yao Wang, and Matteo Mitrano We report the observation of an emergent charge order mode in the optically excited cuprate ladder ${\mathrm{Sr}}_{14}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{24}{\mathrm{O}}_{41}$. Near-infrared light in the ladder plane drives a symmetry-protected electronic metastable state together with a partial melting of the equilibri… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 196501] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: [8] Role of Nonequilibrium Populations in Dark-Exciton Formation
item.description:
Last authors: Stefan Mathias, Marcel Reutzel

Authors: Paul Werner, Wiebke Bennecke, Jan Philipp Bange, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Ultrafast spectroscopy (TR-ARPES relevant authors) on 2D semiconductor materials.

Tags: #2D-materials #spectroscopy #excitons

Abstract: Author(s): Paul Werner, Wiebke Bennecke, Jan Philipp Bange, Giuseppe Meneghini, David Schmitt, Marco Merboldt, Anna M. Seiler, AbdulAziz AlMutairi, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, G. S. Matthijs Jansen, Junde Liu, Daniel Steil, Stephan Hofmann, R. Thomas Weitz, Ermin Malic, Stefan Mathias, and Marcel Reutzel The optical excitation of a bright exciton may be followed by the formation of lower-energy dark states. In these formation and relaxation processes, nonequilibrium exciton and phonon populations play a dominant role but remain so far largely unexplored, as most states are inaccessible by regular sp… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186903] Published Fri May 08, 2026

Thomas Weitz, Ermin Malic, Stefan Mathias, and Marcel Reutzel The optical excitation of a bright exciton may be followed by the formation of lower-energy dark states. item.title: [7] Josephson Effect in Fibonacci Superconductors from Topological Supragap States
item.description:
Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Authors: Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Topological superconductivity and edge modes in quasiperiodic systems.

Tags: #topological-superconductivity #quasicrystals

Abstract: Author(s): Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, and Pablo Burset We theoretically investigate the Josephson effect between two proximized Fibonacci quasicrystals. A quasiperiodic modulation of the chemical potential on a superconducting substrate induces topological gaps and edge modes with energies above the superconducting gap. We reveal that these supragap edg… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186002] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Authors: Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Topological superconductivity and edge modes in quasiperiodic systems.

Tags: #topological-superconductivity #quasicrystals

Abstract: Author(s): Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, and Pablo Burset We theoretically investigate the Josephson effect between two proximized Fibonacci quasicrystals. A quasiperiodic modulation of the chemical potential on a superconducting substrate induces topological gaps and edge modes with energies above the superconducting gap. We reveal that these supragap edg… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186002] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Josephson Effect in Fibonacci Superconductors from Topological Supragap States
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset; Authors: Ignacio Sardinero, Jorge Cayao, Keiji Yada, Yukio Tanaka, Pablo Burset

item.title: [7] Linear Magnetoresistance in a Strange Metal
item.description:
Last authors: Jaewon Kim, Shubhayu Chatterjee

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum criticality and transport in strange metals, relevant to correlated systems.

Tags: #strange-metal #transport #quantum-criticality

Abstract: Author(s): Jaewon Kim and Shubhayu Chatterjee A central puzzle in strongly correlated electronic phases is strange metallic transport, marked by $T$-linear resistivity and $B$-linear magnetoresistance, in sharp contrast with quadratic scalings observed in conventional metals. Here, we demonstrate that proximity to quantum critical points, a rec… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186301] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Jaewon Kim, Shubhayu Chatterjee

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Quantum criticality and transport in strange metals, relevant to correlated systems.

Tags: #strange-metal #transport #quantum-criticality

Abstract: Author(s): Jaewon Kim and Shubhayu Chatterjee A central puzzle in strongly correlated electronic phases is strange metallic transport, marked by $T$-linear resistivity and $B$-linear magnetoresistance, in sharp contrast with quadratic scalings observed in conventional metals. Here, we demonstrate that proximity to quantum critical points, a rec… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186301] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Linear Magnetoresistance in a Strange Metal
item.dc:creator: Jaewon Kim, Shubhayu Chatterjee
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Superconductivity Reinforces Charge-Density-Wave Phase Coherence across Cuprates
item.description:
Last authors: C.-C. Kao, J.-S. Lee

Authors: H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, J.-S. Lee

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct hit on CDW-superconductivity competition in cuprates using resonant scattering.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #cuprates #CDW #superconductivity #RIXS

Abstract: Author(s): H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, and J.-S. Lee For decades, superconductivity in high-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ cuprates has been viewed as a competitor that suppresses charge-density-wave (CDW) order by reducing its amplitude and spatial extent. Here, we show that this picture is incomplete, as superconductivity is accompanied by a systematic enhancem… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186502] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Abstract: Author(s): H. Lee, C.-T. Kuo, M. Fujita, C.-C. Kao, and J.-S. Lee For decades, superconductivity in high-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ cuprates has been viewed as a competitor that suppresses charge-density-wave (CDW) order by reducing its amplitude and spatial extent. Here, we show that this picture is incomplete, as superconductivity is accompanied by a systematic enhancem… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186502] Published Thu May 07, 2026
